title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
subtitle: The Big Ideas Behind Reliable, Scalable, and Maintainable Systems
author: Martin Kleppmann
themes: Reliability, scalability, and maintainability as core design principles
Understanding trade-offs in distributed data systems
The evolution from SQL to NoSQL and back
Consistency models and their practical implications
awards: Jay Kreps: 'bridges the huge gap between theory and practice'
excerpt: Data is at the center of many challenges in system design today. This book helps you navigate the diverse landscape of tools and technologies for processing and storing data by examining the pros and cons of various approaches.
